summary.narrative: Mayor Smiley has delivered on several core campaign promises, particularly around basic city services and securing a landmark PILOT agreement with universities. His 'back-to-basics' approach has shown measurable results in public safety and infrastructure, though some housing and development initiatives remain ongoing.

promises.text: Negotiate a new payment in lieu of taxes (PILOT) agreement with colleges and universities
promises.evidence: Smiley negotiated and signed a historic $223 million PILOT agreement with Providence's colleges and universities, more than doubling previous financial contributions to the city.

promises.text: Launch a Real Time Crime Center to improve public safety
promises.evidence: The Real Time Crime Center was launched and is operational, contributing to public safety monitoring and response capabilities in Providence.
The administration has fully funded the police department, which has contributed to bringing violent crime to a 30-year low according to city reports.
Violent crime has dropped to a 30-year low under the Smiley administration, fulfilling a key public safety promise.

promises.text: Focus on basic city services: fixing potholes, plowing roads, and running the city efficiently
promises.evidence: According to Boston Globe reporting, Smiley has largely accomplished his back-to-basics goals focused on potholes, road plowing, and city efficiency.

promises.text: Pass a comprehensive plan to make building housing easier and faster
promises.evidence: A comprehensive plan has been passed to streamline housing development, with over 1,600 units of housing created or preserved, though full implementation is ongoing.

promises.text: Create or preserve affordable housing units
promises.evidence: The administration reports creating or preserving over 1,600 units of housing, with continued efforts underway to address housing affordability.
